raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The number of unhoused people living on the streets of Los Angeles County increased by 9 percent from 2022 to 2023, reaching an estimated 75,518 people.",
    "The number of unhoused people in the City of Los Angeles rose by 10 percent from 2022 to 2023, totaling an estimated 46,260 people.",
    "The primary reason most people become homeless is economic, with a loss of income being a driving factor.",
    "The average monthly rent in Los Angeles is $2,452, while the median monthly household income preceding homelessness was $935.",
    "People with a history of trauma, people of color, and older adults are at higher risk of becoming homeless.",
    "Most participants in a study believed that rental subsidies, one-time payments, or access to housing vouchers could have prevented homelessness.",
    "Preventing homelessness before it occurs is considered the most cost-effective and compassionate strategy.",
    "AB 1431 aims to provide rent subsidies up to $2,000 a month to the most housing-insecure populations in California.",
    "A two-year rent subsidy program funded at $500 million could prevent homelessness for more than 10,000 people in California.",
    "The California Housing Security Program requires the establishment of a pilot program to develop criteria for housing subsidies for Medi-Cal eligible populations.",
    "AB 1620 seeks to authorize local jurisdictions to allow tenants with permanent physical disabilities to relocate to available accessible units at the same rental rate.",
    "AB 369 allows counties to increase the eligibility age for foster youth to receive support through the Independent Living Program from age 21 to 23.",
    "AB 1335 mandates that planning and reporting for sustainable housing development be more transparent and aligned with housing and sustainability goals."
  ]
}
```
